commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The burden concerning the desert by the sea. As whirlwinds in the south pass through; so it comes from the desert, from a terrible land. The desert of the sea. So Babylon is here called, because from a city as full of people as the sea is with water, it was become a desert. (Challoner) After its fall, it was mostly inundated, chap. Media and Persia, which lay to the south, and were not so beautiful as the environs of Babylon.
